mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks including text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a local-first AI solution with decentralized inference capabilities.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the LocalAI deployment.

```bash

curl -Lo localai https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai

sudo mv localai /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the LocalAI binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development and testing.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with 2 CPUs and 4GB of RAM.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI using a Helm chart, ensuring all necessary resources are created.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI in its own namespace.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks that the LocalAI pods are running correctly in the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the LocalAI namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall the LocalAI Helm release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade LocalAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl uncordon <pod-name>

```

> Drain the LocalAI pods before upgrading.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Uncordon the pods after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure you have internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may be overloaded or unresponsive.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kubernetes cluster and ensure it is healthy.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly or the ingress may not be configured.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and check ingress rules if applicable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.11.x, ensure to update your model configurations to match the new formats.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
